1	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
2	
3	use uv_cache_key::{CanonicalUrl, cache_digest};
4	use uv_distribution_types::IndexUrl;
5	use uv_redacted::DisplaySafeUrl;
6	
7	/// Cache wheels and their metadata, both from remote wheels and built from source distributions.
8	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
9	pub enum WheelCache<'a> {
10	    /// Either PyPI or an alternative index, which we key by index URL.
11	    Index(&'a IndexUrl),
12	    /// A direct URL dependency, which we key by URL.
13	    Url(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),
14	    /// A path dependency, which we key by URL.
15	    Path(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),
16	    /// An editable dependency, which we key by URL.
17	    Editable(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),
18	    /// A Git dependency, which we key by URL (including LFS state), SHA.
19	    ///
20	    /// Note that this variant only exists for source distributions; wheels can't be delivered
21	    /// through Git.
22	    Git(&'a DisplaySafeUrl, &'a str),
23	}
24	
25	impl WheelCache<'_> {
26	    /// The root directory for a cache bucket.
27	    pub fn root(&self) -> PathBuf {
28	        match self {
29	            Self::Index(IndexUrl::Pypi(_)) => WheelCacheKind::Pypi.root(),
30	            Self::Index(url) => WheelCacheKind::Index
31	                .root()
32	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url.url()))),
33	            Self::Url(url) => WheelCacheKind::Url
34	                .root()
35	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url))),
36	            Self::Path(url) => WheelCacheKind::Path
37	                .root()
38	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url))),
39	            Self::Editable(url) => WheelCacheKind::Editable
40	                .root()
41	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url))),
42	            Self::Git(url, sha) => WheelCacheKind::Git
43	                .root()
44	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url)))
45	                .join(sha),
46	        }
47	    }
48	
49	    /// A subdirectory in a bucket for wheels for a specific package.
50	    pub fn wheel_dir(&self, package_name: impl AsRef<Path>) -> PathBuf {
51	        self.root().join(package_name)
52	    }
53	}
54	
55	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
56	pub(crate) enum WheelCacheKind {
57	    /// A cache of data from PyPI.
58	    Pypi,
59	    /// A cache of data from an alternative index.
60	    Index,
61	    /// A cache of data from an arbitrary URL.
62	    Url,
63	    /// A cache of data from a local path.
64	    Path,
65	    /// A cache of data from an editable URL.
66	    Editable,
67	    /// A cache of data from a Git repository.
68	    Git,
69	}
70	
71	impl WheelCacheKind {
72	    pub(crate) fn to_str(self) -> &'static str {
73	        match self {
74	            Self::Pypi => "pypi",
75	            Self::Index => "index",
76	            Self::Url => "url",
77	            Self::Path => "path",
78	            Self::Editable => "editable",
79	            Self::Git => "git",
80	        }
81	    }
82	
83	    pub(crate) fn root(self) -> PathBuf {
84	        Path::new(self.to_str()).to_path_buf()
85	    }
86	}
87	
88	impl AsRef<Path> for WheelCacheKind {
89	    fn as_ref(&self) -> &Path {
90	        self.to_str().as_ref()
91	    }
92	}
93

938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory

940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.

950	    /// # Example
951	    ///

958	    /// ```

968	    /// Ōöé   ...

995	    /// Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64

1033	    Wheels,
1034	    /// Source distributions, wheels built from source distributions, their extracted metadata, and the
1035	    /// cache policy of the source distribution.
1036	    ///
1037	    /// The structure is similar of that of the `Wheel` bucket, except we have an additional layer
1038	    /// for the source distribution filename and the metadata is at the source distribution-level,
1039	    /// not at the wheel level.
1040	    ///
1041	    /// TODO(konstin): The cache policy should be on the source distribution level, the metadata we
1042	    /// can put next to the wheels as in the `Wheels` bucket.
1043	    ///
1044	    /// The unzipped source distribution is stored in a directory matching the source distribution
1045	    /// archive name.
1046	    ///
1047	    /// Source distributions are built into zipped wheel files (as PEP 517 specifies) and unzipped
1048	    /// lazily before installing. So when resolving, we only build the wheel and store the archive
1049	    /// file in the cache, when installing, we unpack it under the same name (exclusive of the
1050	    /// `.whl` extension). You may find a mix of wheel archive zip files and unzipped wheel
1051	    /// directories in the cache.
1052	    ///
1053	    /// Cache structure:
1054	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/pypi/foo/34a17436ed1e9669/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0.zip, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1055	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1056	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1057	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/git/<digest(url)>/<git sha>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1058	    ///
1059	    /// But the url filename does not need to be a valid source dist filename
1060	    /// (<https://github.com/search?q=path%3A**%2Frequirements.txt+master.zip&type=code>),
1061	    /// so it could also be the following and we have to take any string as filename:
1062	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<sha256(url)>/master.zip/metadata.msgpack`
1063	    ///
1064	    /// # Example
1065	    ///
1066	    /// The following requirements:
1067	    /// ```text
1068	    /// # git source dist

1074	    /// ```

1079	    /// Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ git
1080	    /// Ōöé┬Ā┬Ā ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ 2122faf3e081fb7a

1083	    /// Ōöé┬Ā┬Ā ┬Ā ┬Ā     ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ pydantic_extra_types-2.9.0-py3-none-any.whl
1084	    /// Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pypi
1085	    /// Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ django-allauth
1086	    /// Ōöé       ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ 0.51.0
1087	    /// Ōöé           Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ 0gH-_fwv8tdJ7JwwjJsUc
1088	    /// Ōöé           Ōöé┬Ā┬Ā Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ django-allauth-0.51.0.tar.gz
1089	    /// Ōöé           Ōöé   Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ [UNZIPPED CONTENTS]

1121	    ///

1123	    ///

1204	    /// Remove a package from the cache bucket.
1205	    ///
1206	    /// Returns the number of entries removed from the cache.
1207	    fn remove(self, cache: &Cache, name: &PackageName) -> Result<Removal, io::Error> {
1208	        /// Returns `true` if the [`Path`] represents a built wheel for the given package.
1209	        fn is_match(path: &Path, name: &PackageName) -> bool {
1210	            let Ok(metadata) = fs_err::read(path.join("metadata.msgpack")) else {
1211	                return false;
1212	            };
1213	            let Ok(metadata) = rmp_serde::from_slice::<ResolutionMetadata>(&metadata) else {
1214	                return false;
1215	            };
1216	            metadata.name == *name
1217	        }
1218	
1219	        let mut summary = Removal::default();
1220	        match self {
1221	            Self::Wheels => {
1222	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a directory per package (indexed by name).
1223	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1224	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(name.to_string()))?;
1225	
1226	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1227	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1228	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1229	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1230	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1231	                }
1232	
1233	                // For direct URLs, we expect a directory for every URL, followed by a
1234	                // directory per package (indexed by name).
1235	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Url);
1236	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1237	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1238	                }
1239	            }
1240	            Self::SourceDistributions => {
1241	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a directory per package (indexed by name).
1242	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1243	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(name.to_string()))?;
1244	
1245	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1246	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1247	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1248	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1249	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1250	                }
1251	
1252	                // For direct URLs, we expect a directory for every URL, followed by a
1253	                // directory per version. To determine whether the URL is relevant, we need to
1254	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1255	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Url);
1256	                for url in directories(root)? {
1257	                    if directories(&url)?.any(|version| is_match(&version, name)) {
1258	                        summary += rm_rf(url)?;
1259	                    }
1260	                }
1261	
1262	                // For local dependencies, we expect a directory for every path, followed by a
1263	                // directory per version. To determine whether the path is relevant, we need to
1264	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1265	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Path);
1266	                for path in directories(root)? {
1267	                    if directories(&path)?.any(|version| is_match(&version, name)) {
1268	                        summary += rm_rf(path)?;
1269	                    }
1270	                }
1271	
1272	                // For Git dependencies, we expect a directory for every repository, followed by a
1273	                // directory for every SHA. To determine whether the SHA is relevant, we need to
1274	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1275	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Git);
1276	                for repository in directories(root)? {
1277	                    for sha in directories(repository)? {
1278	                        if is_match(&sha, name) {
1279	                            summary += rm_rf(sha)?;
1280	                        }
1281	                    }
1282	                }
1283	            }
1284	            Self::Simple => {
1285	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a rkyv file per package, indexed by name.
1286	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1287	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(format!("{name}.rkyv")))?;
1288	
1289	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1290	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1291	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1292	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1293	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(format!("{name}.rkyv")))?;
1294	                }
1295	            }
1296	            Self::FlatIndex => {
1297	                // We can't know if the flat index includes a package, so we just remove the entire
1298	                // cache entry.
1299	                let root = cache.bucket(self);

1360	/// A refresh policy for cache entries.
1361	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
1362	pub enum Refresh {
1363	    /// Don't refresh any entries.
1364	    None(Timestamp),
1365	    /// Refresh entries linked to the given packages, if created before the given timestamp.
1366	    Packages(Vec<PackageName>, Vec<Box<Path>>, Timestamp),
1367	    /// Refresh all entries created before the given timestamp.
1368	    All(Timestamp),
1369	}
1370	
1371	impl Refresh {
1372	    /// Determine the refresh strategy to use based on the command-line arguments.
1373	    pub fn from_args(refresh: Option<bool>, refresh_package: Vec<PackageName>) -> Self {
1374	        let timestamp = Timestamp::now();
1375	        match refresh {
1376	            Some(true) => Self::All(timestamp),
1377	            Some(false) => Self::None(timestamp),
1378	            None => {
1379	                if refresh_package.is_empty() {
1380	                    Self::None(timestamp)
1381	                } else {
1382	                    Self::Packages(refresh_package, vec![], timestamp)
1383	                }
1384	            }
1385	        }
1386	    }
1387	
1388	    /// Return the [`Timestamp`] associated with the refresh policy.
1389	    pub fn timestamp(&self) -> Timestamp {
1390	        match self {
1391	            Self::None(timestamp) => *timestamp,
1392	            Self::Packages(.., timestamp) => *timestamp,
1393	            Self::All(timestamp) => *timestamp,
1394	        }
1395	    }
1396	
1397	    /// Returns `true` if no packages should be reinstalled.
1398	    pub fn is_none(&self) -> bool {
1399	        matches!(self, Self::None(_))
1400	    }
1401	
1402	    /// Combine two [`Refresh`] policies, taking the "max" of the two policies.
1403	    #[must_use]
1404	    pub fn combine(self, other: Self) -> Self {
1405	        match (self, other) {
1406	            // If the policy is `None`, return the existing refresh policy.
1407	            // Take the `max` of the two timestamps.
1408	            (Self::None(t1), Self::None(t2)) => Self::None(t1.max(t2)),
1409	            (Self::None(t1), Self::All(t2)) => Self::All(t1.max(t2)),
1410	            (Self::None(t1), Self::Packages(packages, paths, t2)) => {
1411	                Self::Packages(packages, paths, t1.max(t2))
1412	            }
1413	
1414	            // If the policy is `All`, refresh all packages.
1415	            (Self::All(t1), Self::None(t2) | Self::All(t2) | Self::Packages(.., t2)) => {
1416	                Self::All(t1.max(t2))
1417	            }
1418	
1419	            // If the policy is `Packages`, take the "max" of the two policies.
1420	            (Self::Packages(packages, paths, t1), Self::None(t2)) => {
1421	                Self::Packages(packages, paths, t1.max(t2))
1422	            }
1423	            (Self::Packages(.., t1), Self::All(t2)) => Self::All(t1.max(t2)),
1424	            (Self::Packages(packages1, paths1, t1), Self::Packages(packages2, paths2, t2)) => {
1425	                Self::Packages(
1426	                    packages1.into_iter().chain(packages2).collect(),
1427	                    paths1.into_iter().chain(paths2).collect(),
1428	                    t1.max(t2),
1429	                )
1430	            }
1431	        }
1432	    }
1433	}

10	
11	/// When the cache directory cannot be created (e.g., due to permissions), we should show a
12	/// chained error message that indicates we failed to initialize the cache.
13	#[test]
14	#[cfg(unix)]
15	fn cache_init_failure() -> Result<()> {
16	    use uv_test::ReadOnlyDirectoryGuard;
17	
18	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
19	
20	    let pyproject_toml = context.temp_dir.child("pyproject.toml");
21	    pyproject_toml.write_str(
22	        r#"
23	        [project]
24	        name = "project"
25	        version = "0.1.0"
26	        requires-python = ">=3.12"
27	        dependencies = ["iniconfig"]
28	        "#,
29	    )?;
30	
31	    // Create a read-only directory that will serve as the parent of the cache.
32	    // The guard sets it to read-only and restores original permissions on drop (including panic).
33	    let cache_parent = context.temp_dir.child("cache_parent");
34	    fs_err::create_dir(&cache_parent)?;
35	    let _guard = ReadOnlyDirectoryGuard::new(cache_parent.path())?;
36	
37	    // Point the cache to a subdirectory within the read-only parent
38	    let cache_dir = cache_parent.child("cache");
39	
40	    // Filter both the relative path (in the first line) and absolute path (in the cause)
41	    let context = context
42	        .with_filter((r"cache_parent/cache", "[CACHE_DIR]"))
43	        .with_filter((
44	            r"failed to create directory `.*`",
45	            "failed to create directory `[CACHE_DIR]`",
46	        ));
47	
48	    // Build the sync command manually to use our custom cache directory.
49	    // We can't use context.sync() because it adds --cache-dir with the default cache.
50	    let mut command = Command::new(get_bin!());
51	    command
52	        .arg("sync")
53	        .arg("--cache-dir")
54	        .arg(cache_dir.path())
55	        .current_dir(context.temp_dir.path());
56	    context.add_shared_env(&mut command, false);
57	
58	    // Running a command should fail with a chained error about cache initialization
59	    uv_snapshot!(context.filters(), command, @"
60	    success: false
61	    exit_code: 2
62	    ----- stdout -----
63	
64	    ----- stderr -----
65	    error: Failed to initialize cache at `[CACHE_DIR]`
66	      Caused by: failed to create directory `[CACHE_DIR]`: Permission denied (os error 13)
67	    ");
68	
69	    Ok(())
70	}
71

434	    /// Manage Python versions and installations
435	    ///
436	    /// Generally, uv first searches for Python in a virtual environment, either active or in a
437	    /// `.venv` directory in the current working directory or any parent directory. If a virtual
438	    /// environment is not required, uv will then search for a Python interpreter. Python
439	    /// interpreters are found by searching for Python executables in the `PATH` environment
440	    /// variable.
441	    ///
442	    /// On Windows, the registry is also searched for Python executables.
443	    ///
444	    /// By default, uv will download Python if a version cannot be found. This behavior can be
445	    /// disabled with the `--no-python-downloads` flag or the `python-downloads` setting.
446	    ///
447	    /// The `--python` option allows requesting a different interpreter.
448	    ///
449	    /// The following Python version request formats are supported:
450	    ///
451	    /// - `<version>` e.g. `3`, `3.12`, `3.12.3`
452	    /// - `<version-specifier>` e.g. `>=3.12,<3.13`
453	    /// - `<version><short-variant>` (e.g., `3.13t`, `3.12.0d`)
454	    /// - `<version>+<variant>` (e.g., `3.13+freethreaded`, `3.12.0+debug`)
455	    /// - `<implementation>` e.g. `cpython` or `cp`
456	    /// - `<implementation>@<version>` e.g. `cpython@3.12`
457	    /// - `<implementation><version>` e.g. `cpython3.12` or `cp312`
458	    /// - `<implementation><version-specifier>` e.g. `cpython>=3.12,<3.13`
459	    /// - `<implementation>-<version>-<os>-<arch>-<libc>` e.g. `cpython-3.12.3-macos-aarch64-none`
460	    ///
461	    /// Additionally, a specific system Python interpreter can often be requested with:
462	    ///
463	    /// - `<executable-path>` e.g. `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3`
464	    /// - `<executable-name>` e.g. `mypython3`
465	    /// - `<install-dir>` e.g. `/some/environment/`
466	    ///
467	    /// When the `--python` option is used, normal discovery rules apply but discovered interpreters
468	    /// are checked for compatibility with the request, e.g., if `pypy` is requested, uv will first
469	    /// check if the virtual environment contains a PyPy interpreter then check if each executable
470	    /// in the path is a PyPy interpreter.
471	    ///
472	    /// uv supports discovering CPython, PyPy, and GraalPy interpreters. Unsupported interpreters
473	    /// will be skipped during discovery. If an unsupported interpreter implementation is requested,
474	    /// uv will exit with an error.

878	#[derive(Subcommand)]
879	pub enum CacheCommand {
880	    /// Clear the cache, removing all entries or those linked to specific packages.
881	    #[command(alias = "clear")]
882	    Clean(CleanArgs),
883	    /// Prune all unreachable objects from the cache.
884	    Prune(PruneArgs),
885	    /// Show the cache directory.
886	    ///
887	    /// By default, the cache is stored in `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/uv` or `$HOME/.cache/uv` on Unix and
888	    /// `%LOCALAPPDATA%\uv\cache` on Windows.
889	    ///
890	    /// When `--no-cache` is used, the cache is stored in a temporary directory and discarded when
891	    /// the process exits.
892	    ///
893	    /// An alternative cache directory may be specified via the `cache-dir` setting, the
894	    /// `--cache-dir` option, or the `$UV_CACHE_DIR` environment variable.
895	    ///
896	    /// Note that it is important for performance for the cache directory to be located on the same
897	    /// file system as the Python environment uv is operating on.
898	    Dir,
899	    /// Show the cache size.
900	    ///

Now I have comprehensive information about the cache structure. Let me create a detailed report:

## Cache Bucket and Directory Structure - Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the uv codebase and compiled a comprehensive analysis of the cache system. Here's what I found:

### 1. Cache Buckets (Defined in `CacheBucket` Enum)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` (lines 934-1177)

The system defines **12 cache buckets** as an enum:

| Bucket Variant | Disk Name | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Wheels` | `wheels-v6` | Pre-built wheels from PyPI/indices, including metadata and unzipped archives |
| `SourceDistributions` | `sdists-v9` | Source distributions, built wheels, extracted metadata, and cache policies |
| `FlatIndex` | `flat-index-v2` | Flat index responses (similar to simple metadata API), stored as msgpack |
| `Git` | `git-v0` | Git repositories |
| `Interpreter` | `interpreter-v4` | Python interpreter info at a path (PEP 508 markers, sys/sysconfig data) |
| `Simple` | `simple-v21` | Index responses through simple metadata API (stored as rkyv format) |
| `Archive` | `archive-v0` | Unzipped wheels stored as directories (internal use via symlinks from other buckets) |
| `Builds` | `builds-v0` | Ephemeral venvs for PEP 517 builds |
| `Environments` | `environments-v2` | Reusable venvs for invoking Python tools |
| `Python` | `python-v0` | Cached Python downloads |
| `Binaries` | `binaries-v0` | Downloaded tool binaries (e.g., Ruff) |

### 2. Cache Sub-Bucket Structure (Wheel Cache Kinds)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv-cache/src/wheel.rs` (lines 56-92)

For `Wheels` and `SourceDistributions` buckets, there are sub-categories defined by `WheelCacheKind` enum:

| Sub-Bucket | Directory Name | Use Case |
|---|---|---|
| `Pypi` | `pypi` | PyPI packages (default index) |
| `Index` | `index` | Alternative package indices |
| `Url` | `url` | Direct URL dependencies |
| `Path` | `path` | Local path dependencies |
| `Editable` | `editable` | Editable dependencies |
| `Git` | `git` | Git repository dependencies |

### 3. Directory Structure Examples

**For Wheels (from lib.rs comments, lines 943-1033):**
```
wheels-v6/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pypi/
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pandas/
Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
Ōöé   Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64/  [unzipped]
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ ...
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ index/
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <digest(index-url)>/
Ōöé       ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <package>/
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ url/
    ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <digest(url)>/
        ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <package>/
```

**For Source Distributions (from lib.rs comments, lines 1078-1101):**
```
sdists-v9/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ git/
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <digest(url)>/
Ōöé       ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <git-sha>/
Ōöé           Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ metadata.msgpack
Ōöé           ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <package>-version-py3-none-any.whl
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ pypi/
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <package>/
Ōöé       ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <version>/
Ōöé           Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ <build-id>/
Ōöé           Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ <source.tar.gz>
Ōöé           Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ metadata.msgpack
Ōöé           Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <package>-version-py3-none-any.whl
Ōöé           ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ revision.http
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ url/
    ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <digest(url)>/
        Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ <build-id>/
        Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ metadata.msgpack
        Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ <package>-version-py3-none-any.whl
        ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ revision.http
```

### 4. Core Cache Structures

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

Core structs (lines 40-177):

1. **`Cache` struct (line 164)**: Main cache abstraction
   - `root: PathBuf` - cache directory
   - `refresh: Refresh` - refresh strategy
   - `temp_dir: Option<Arc<tempfile::TempDir>>` - temporary directory if `--no-cache`
   - `lock_file: Option<Arc<LockedFile>>` - read lock for concurrent safety

2. **`CacheEntry` struct (line 53)**: Single cache entry (file)
   - `PathBuf` wrapper around cache file path
   - Methods: `lock()`, `path()`, `dir()`, `shard()`, `with_file()`

3. **`CacheShard` struct (line 115)**: Subdirectory within cache
   - `PathBuf` wrapper
   - Methods: `entry()`, `shard()`, `lock()`

4. **`Removal` struct (removal.rs, lines 44-55)**: Statistics for cache removal
   - `num_files: u64`
   - `num_dirs: u64`
   - `total_bytes: u64`

5. **`ArchiveId` struct (archive.rs, lines 4-40)**: Unique identifier for unzipped wheels
   - Uses `nanoid` for random IDs

### 5. Cache Size Measurement

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`

Current implementation (lines 13-47):
- Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` crate to measure total cache size
- **Note:** Currently computes **only total cache size**, not per-bucket sizes
- Used by `uv cache size` command
- Returns: `total_bytes` (option to display as human-readable)

### 6. Cache Cleanup Operations

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs` (lines 15-107)

The `cache_clean()` function:
- Clears entire cache or removes entries for specific packages
- Iterates through all buckets: `for bucket in CacheBucket::iter()`
- Returns `Removal` stats (files, dirs, bytes removed)
- Handles concurrency with exclusive locks

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_prune.rs` (lines 13-94)

The `cache_prune()` function:
- Removes unreachable objects and orphaned archives
- For CI mode: removes unzipped wheels and built wheel intermediates
- Calls `cache.prune(ci)` which handles archive references

### 7. File Organization Summary

**uv-cache crate files:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `lib.rs` | Main cache abstraction: `Cache`, `CacheEntry`, `CacheShard` structs; `CacheBucket` enum; cache operations |
| `wheel.rs` | `WheelCache` enum (for specific wheel/source dist resolution) and `WheelCacheKind` sub-categories |
| `archive.rs` | `ArchiveId` struct for unzipped wheel directories |
| `removal.rs` | `Removal` struct and `rm_rf()` implementation for cache cleanup |
| `by_timestamp.rs` | `CachedByTimestamp<T>` wrapper for caching timestamped data |
| `cli.rs` | `CacheArgs` clap arguments for cache configuration |

### 8. Key Implementation Details

**Version Numbering:** Each bucket has a version suffix (e.g., `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`). When bumped, these must be updated in multiple locations:
- `lib.rs` (main definition)
- `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_clean.rs`
- `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_prune.rs`

**Archive Constant:**
```rust
pub const ARCHIVE_VERSION: u8 = 0;  // line 36
```

**Bucket Iteration:**
```rust
pub fn iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {  // lines 1316-1331
    [Self::Wheels, Self::SourceDistributions, Self::FlatIndex, 
     Self::Git, Self::Interpreter, Self::Simple, Self::Archive, 
     Self::Builds, Self::Environments, Self::Binaries]
    .iter().copied()
}
```

### 9. Important Observations

1. **No per-bucket size tracking:** The current `cache_size` implementation measures only total cache size using `diskus::DiskUsage`. There is no code that computes sizes per bucket.

2. **Python bucket is new:** The `Python` bucket is present in the enum but not included in the `iter()` method (line 1327), suggesting it may be newer/optional.

3. **Archive handling:** Archives are stored separately and referenced via symlinks (Unix) or special `.link` files (Windows) for atomic operations.

4. **Lock mechanisms:** Cache uses `.lock` files and shared/exclusive locking to prevent concurrent modification issues.

5. **Removal tracking:** All cache removal operations return `Removal` stats with files, directories, and byte countsŌĆöthis is the only place where per-operation size metrics are tracked.

Current implementation (lines 13-47):
- Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` crate to measure total cache size
- **Note:** Currently computes **only total cache size**, not per-bucket sizes
- Used by `uv cache size` command
- Returns: `total_bytes` (option to display as human-readable)

### 6. Cache Cleanup Operations

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs` (lines 15-107)

The `cache_clean()` function:
- Clears entire cache or removes entries for specific packages
- Iterates through all buckets: `for bucket in CacheBucket::iter()`
- Returns `Removal` stats (files, dirs, bytes removed)
- Handles concurrency with exclusive locks

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_prune.rs` (lines 13-94)

The `cache_prune()` function:
- Removes unreachable objects and orphaned archives
- For CI mode: removes unzipped wheels and built wheel intermediates
- Calls `cache.prune(ci)` which handles archive references

### 7. File Organization Summary

**uv-cache crate files:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `lib.rs` | Main cache abstraction: `Cache`, `CacheEntry`, `CacheShard` structs; `CacheBucket` enum; cache operations |
| `wheel.rs` | `WheelCache` enum (for specific wheel/source dist resolution) and `WheelCacheKind` sub-categories |
| `archive.rs` | `ArchiveId` struct for unzipped wheel directories |
| `removal.rs` | `Removal` struct and `rm_rf()` implementation for cache cleanup |
| `by_timestamp.rs` | `CachedByTimestamp<T>` wrapper for caching timestamped data |
| `cli.rs` | `CacheArgs` clap arguments for cache configuration |

### 8. Key Implementation Details

**Version Numbering:** Each bucket has a version suffix (e.g., `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`). When bumped, these must be updated in multiple locations:
- `lib.rs` (main definition)

```rust
pub fn iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {  // lines 1316-1331

    .iter().copied()
}
```

### 9. Important Observations

1. **No per-bucket size tracking:** The current `cache_size` implementation measures only total cache size using `diskus::DiskUsage`. There is no code that computes sizes per bucket.

## Comprehensive Report: UV Cache Commands Implementation

### 1. CLI ARGUMENT PARSING FOR CACHE SUBCOMMANDS

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`

- **Line 879-905**: `CacheCommand` enum - defines all cache subcommands:

  #[derive(Subcommand)]
  pub enum CacheCommand {
      /// Clear the cache, removing all entries or those linked to specific packages.
      #[command(alias = "clear")]
      Clean(CleanArgs),
      /// Prune all unreachable objects from the cache.
      Prune(PruneArgs),
      /// Show the cache directory.
      Dir,
      /// Show the cache size.
      ///
      /// Displays the total size of the cache directory. This includes all downloaded and built
      /// wheels, source distributions, and other cached data. By default, outputs the size in raw
      /// bytes; use `--human` for human-readable output.
      Size(SizeArgs),
  }
  ```

- **Line 948-952**: `SizeArgs` struct - arguments for the `cache size` subcommand:

  #[derive(Args, Debug)]
  pub struct SizeArgs {
      /// Display the cache size in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB` instead of raw bytes).
      #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
      pub human: bool,
  }
  ```

- **Line 908-918**: `CleanArgs` struct - arguments for the `cache clean` subcommand:
  ```rust
  #[derive(Args, Debug)]
  pub struct CleanArgs {
      /// The packages to remove from the cache.
      #[arg(value_hint = ValueHint::Other)]
      pub package: Vec<PackageName>,
      
      /// Force removal of the cache, ignoring in-use checks.

  ```

- **Line 922-945**: `PruneArgs` struct - arguments for the `cache prune` subcommand with `--ci` flag support.

- **Line 553**: Integration in main `Commands` enum:

### 2. COMMAND DISPATCH AND EXECUTION

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/lib.rs`

**Dispatch Pattern (Lines 1163-1181):**

```rust
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Clean(args),
})
| Commands::Clean(args) => {
    show_settings!(args);
    commands::cache_clean(&args.package, args.force, cache, printer).await
}
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Prune(args),
}) => {
    show_settings!(args);
    commands::cache_prune(args.ci, args.force, cache, printer).await
}
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Dir,
}) => commands::cache_dir(&cache, printer),
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Size(args),
}) => commands::cache_size(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
```

The dispatcher pattern:
1. Matches on `Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace { command: ... })`
2. Extracts the specific variant (Clean, Prune, Dir, or Size)
3. Calls the corresponding command handler function

### 3. CACHE SIZE IMPLEMENTATION

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`

pub(crate) fn cache_size(

```

**Implementation Details:**
- **Line 19-24**: Checks if the `CacheSize` preview feature is enabled, warns if experimental
- **Line 26-33**: Returns "0" or "0B" if cache directory doesn't exist
- **Line 35**: Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` to calculate directory size:

### 6. OTHER CACHE SUBCOMMANDS FOR REFERENCE

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_dir.rs`

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs`
- Handles cache cleaning with optional package filtering

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_prune.rs`

### 7. TESTS

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`

Key test patterns:

- `cache_size_with_packages_raw()`: Verifies positive byte count after package installation
- `cache_size_with_packages_human()`: Tests human-readable format with `--human` flag

**Summary of Control Flow:**
1. User runs `uv cache size [--human]`
2. Clap parses arguments into `CacheNamespace { command: CacheCommand::Size(SizeArgs { human: bool }) }`

## Summary Report: uv Codebase Architecture

Based on my exploration of the uv codebase, here are my detailed findings:

---

### 1. **End-to-End CLI Wiring for New Subcommands**

The path from CLI argument definition to command execution follows this flow:

**A. Argument Definition**
- Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`
- Lines 110-116: The top-level `Cli` struct with `#[derive(Parser)]` using clap
- Lines 873-905: The `CacheNamespace` struct containing `CacheCommand` enum with subcommands like `Size(SizeArgs)`, `Clean(CleanArgs)`, `Prune(PruneArgs)`, `Dir`
- Lines 948-952: The `SizeArgs` struct defines the `--human` / `-H` flag:
```rust
pub struct SizeArgs {
    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
    pub human: bool,
}
```

**B. Command Routing & Execution**
- Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/lib.rs`
- Lines 1179-1181: The cache size command is dispatched:
```rust
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Size(args),
}) => commands::cache_size(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
```

---

### 2. **`uv cache size` Output Display Code**

The actual printing happens in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs` (lines 13-47):

```rust
pub(crate) fn cache_size(
    cache: &Cache,
    human_readable: bool,
    printer: Printer,
    preview: Preview,
) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    // ... preview warning check ...
    
    if !cache.root().exists() {
        if human_readable {
            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "0B")?;
        } else {
            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "0")?;
        }
        return Ok(ExitStatus::Success);
    }

    let disk_usage = DiskUsage::new(vec![cache.root().to_path_buf()]);
    let total_bytes = disk_usage.count_ignoring_errors();

    if human_readable {
        let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
    } else {
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
    }

    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
}
```

**Key observations:**
- Uses `printer.stdout_important()` for output (respects quiet/silent modes)
- Uses the `diskus` crate to calculate directory size
- Calls `human_readable_bytes()` for formatting when `--human` flag is set
- Outputs with `.1` precision (one decimal place) for human-readable format

---

### 3. **Human-Readable Size Formatting Utility**

Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs` (lines 309-314):

```rust
pub fn human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str) {
    const UNITS: [&str; 7] = ["B", "KiB", "MiB", "GiB", "TiB", "PiB", "EiB"];
    let bytes_f32 = bytes as f32;
    let i = ((bytes_f32.log2() / 10.0) as usize).min(UNITS.len() - 1);
    (bytes_f32 / 1024_f32.powi(i as i32), UNITS[i])
}
```

**Returns:** A tuple of `(f32, &'static str)` - the numeric value and unit string. This is imported and re-exported in `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs` line 65:
```rust
pub(crate) use uv_console::human_readable_bytes;
```

This utility is also used by `cache_clean` and `cache_prune` commands (e.g., lines 98-100 in cache_clean.rs):
```rust
let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(summary.total_bytes);
format!("{bytes:.1}{unit}")
```

---

### 4. **Workspace & GlobalArgs Patterns**

**GlobalArgs Pattern** (`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` lines 158+):
- Defined as a `#[derive(Parser)]` struct with `#[command(next_help_heading = "Global options")]`
- Includes global flags like `--verbose`, `--quiet`, `--color`, `--preview`, `--offline`, etc.
- Used in command execution context to access settings like `globals.preview`

**Workspace Pattern** (accessed in `crates/uv/src/lib.rs` line 246):
```rust
Workspace::discover(&project_dir, &DiscoveryOptions::default(), &workspace_cache).await
```
- Not used for simple cache commands like `cache size` (which are not project-aware)
- Used for project-related commands that need workspace discovery
- Utilizes a `WorkspaceCache` for efficiency (line 229)

**Printer Pattern** (from `crates/uv/src/printer.rs` lines 5-77):
```rust
pub(crate) enum Printer {
    Silent, Quiet, Default, Verbose, NoProgress,
}
```
- Provides output control with methods like `stdout()`, `stdout_important()`, `stderr()`
- `stdout_important()` writes to stdout even in Quiet mode
- `stdout()` is suppressed in Quiet/Silent modes
- Passed to all command functions for consistent output handling

---

### 5. **Integration Tests for Cache Commands**

Located in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477387363-bhvxwr/crates/uv/tests/it/`:

**cache_size.rs** - Tests for `uv cache size`:
- `cache_size_empty_raw()` - Tests raw output (bytes) for empty cache
- `cache_size_with_packages_raw()` - Tests raw byte output after installing packages
- `cache_size_with_packages_human()` - Tests `--human` flag output after installing packages

**cache_clean.rs** - Tests for `uv cache clean`:
- `clean_all()` - Tests removing all cache entries
- `clear_all_alias()` - Tests `cache clear` as alias for `cache clean`
- `clean_force()` - Tests `--force` flag behavior
- `clean_package_pypi()` - Tests removing specific packages
- `cache_timeout()` - Tests lock timeout behavior

**cache_prune.rs** - Tests for `uv cache prune`

**cache.rs** - General cache functionality:
- `cache_init_failure()` - Tests error handling when cache directory creation fails

**Test Infrastructure** (`crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`):
- Lines 241-251: `with_filtered_cache_size()` method filters cache size output:
```rust
pub fn with_filtered_cache_size(mut self) -> Self {
    // Filter raw byte counts
    self.filters.push((r"(?m)^\d+\n".to_string(), "[SIZE]\n".to_string()));
    // Filter human-readable sizes (e.g., "384.2 KiB")
    self.filters.push((
        r"(?m)^\d+(\.\d+)? [KMGT]i?B\n".to_string(),
        "[SIZE]\n".to_string(),
    ));
    self
}
```

- Lines 1644-1649: `cache_size()` test helper creates the command:
```rust
pub fn cache_size(&self) -> Command {
    let mut command = self.new_command();
    command.arg("cache").arg("size");
    self.add_shared_options(&mut command, false);
    command
}
```

---

### 6. **Key Files Summary**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` | CLI argument definitions (Cli, CacheCommand, SizeArgs) |
| `crates/uv/src/lib.rs` | Main execution logic and command routing |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs` | Implementation of `uv cache size` |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs` | Implementation of `uv cache clean` |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_prune.rs` | Implementation of `uv cache prune` |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_dir.rs` | Implementation of `uv cache dir` |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs` | Command module exports and utilities |
| `crates/uv/src/printer.rs` | Output control system |
| `crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs` | `human_readable_bytes()` utility function |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs` | Integration tests for cache size |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_clean.rs` | Integration tests for cache clean |
| `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs` | Test infrastructure and helpers |

---

### 7. **Architecture Patterns Observed**

1. **Clap-based CLI**: Uses `#[derive(Parser)]` from clap for argument parsing
2. **Enum-based command routing**: Commands dispatched through pattern matching on enum variants
3. **Per-command function pattern**: Each command (cache_size, cache_clean, etc.) gets its own function
4. **Result-based error handling**: Uses `Result<ExitStatus>` return type
5. **Printer abstraction**: Centralized output control through Printer enum
6. **Human formatting utility**: Single utility for human-readable byte formatting
7. **Snapshot testing**: Uses snapshot tests with output filtering for cache size tests

pub fn with_filtered_cache_size(mut self) -> Self {
    // Filter raw byte counts

    // Filter human-readable sizes (e.g., "384.2 KiB")
    self.filters.push((

}

### 7. **Architecture Patterns Observed**

1. **Clap-based CLI**: Uses `#[derive(Parser)]` from clap for argument parsing
2. **Enum-based command routing**: Commands dispatched through pattern matching on enum variants
3. **Per-command function pattern**: Each command (cache_size, cache_clean, etc.) gets its own function
4. **Result-based error handling**: Uses `Result<ExitStatus>` return type
5. **Printer abstraction**: Centralized output control through Printer enum
6. **Human formatting utility**: Single utility for human-readable byte formatting
7. **Snapshot testing**: Uses snapshot tests with output filtering for cache size tests

888	    /// `%LOCALAPPDATA%\uv\cache` on Windows.
889	    ///
890	    /// When `--no-cache` is used, the cache is stored in a temporary directory and discarded when
891	    /// the process exits.
892	    ///
893	    /// An alternative cache directory may be specified via the `cache-dir` setting, the
894	    /// `--cache-dir` option, or the `$UV_CACHE_DIR` environment variable.
895	    ///
896	    /// Note that it is important for performance for the cache directory to be located on the same
897	    /// file system as the Python environment uv is operating on.

160	/// The main cache abstraction.
161	///
162	/// While the cache is active, it holds a read (shared) lock that prevents cache cleaning
163	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
164	pub struct Cache {
165	    /// The cache directory.
166	    root: PathBuf,
167	    /// The refresh strategy to use when reading from the cache.
168	    refresh: Refresh,
169	    /// A temporary cache directory, if the user requested `--no-cache`.
170	    ///
171	    /// Included to ensure that the temporary directory exists for the length of the operation, but
172	    /// is dropped at the end as appropriate.
173	    temp_dir: Option<Arc<tempfile::TempDir>>,
174	    /// Ensure that `uv cache` operations don't remove items from the cache that are used by another
175	    /// uv process.
176	    lock_file: Option<Arc<LockedFile>>,
177	}
178	
179	impl Cache {
180	    /// A persistent cache directory at `root`.
181	    pub fn from_path(root: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self {
182	        Self {
183	            root: root.into(),
184	            refresh: Refresh::None(Timestamp::now()),
185	            temp_dir: None,
186	            lock_file: None,
187	        }
188	    }
189	
190	    /// Create a temporary cache directory.
191	    pub fn temp() -> Result<Self, io::Error> {
192	        let temp_dir = tempfile::tempdir()?;
193	        Ok(Self {
194	            root: temp_dir.path().to_path_buf(),
195	            refresh: Refresh::None(Timestamp::now()),
196	            temp_dir: Some(Arc::new(temp_dir)),
197	            lock_file: None,
198	        })
199	    }
200	
201	    /// Set the [`Refresh`] policy for the cache.
202	    #[must_use]
203	    pub fn with_refresh(self, refresh: Refresh) -> Self {
204	        Self { refresh, ..self }
205	    }
206	
207	    /// Acquire a lock that allows removing entries from the cache.
208	    pub async fn with_exclusive_lock(self) -> Result<Self, LockedFileError> {
209	        let Self {
210	            root,
211	            refresh,
212	            temp_dir,
213	            lock_file,
214	        } = self;
215	
216	        // Release the existing lock, avoid deadlocks from a cloned cache.
217	        if let Some(lock_file) = lock_file {
218	            drop(
219	                Arc::try_unwrap(lock_file).expect(
220	                    "cloning the cache before acquiring an exclusive lock causes a deadlock",
221	                ),
222	            );
223	        }
224	        let lock_file = LockedFile::acquire(
225	            root.join(".lock"),
226	            LockedFileMode::Exclusive,
227	            root.simplified_display(),
228	        )
229	        .await?;
230	
231	        Ok(Self {
232	            root,
233	            refresh,
234	            temp_dir,
235	            lock_file: Some(Arc::new(lock_file)),
236	        })
237	    }
238

Actually, I have enough information to write the plan. Let me now write it.

Here's the summary of what I need to do:

1. **`crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`**: Add `CacheStats = 1 << 28` variant to `PreviewFeature` enum, add `"cache-stats"` to `as_str()` and `FromStr` impls.


2. **`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`**: Add a `Stats` variant to `CacheCommand` enum with a corresponding `StatsArgs` struct that includes a `human` field for formatting output.
3. **`crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs`**: Create the main command handler that checks the preview feature, iterates through cache buckets, and computes disk usage for each one.
4. **`crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`**: Wire up the new module and export the function.
5. **`crates/uv/src/lib.rs`**: Add the dispatch logic for the stats command.
6. **`crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`**: Add a test helper for the cache stats command.
7. **`crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`** or a new test file: Write integration tests to verify the output format and calculations.

Now I'm thinking about the output formatŌĆölooking at how `cache size` is structured, I want to make sure the stats command displays the data in a consistent way.

Generating output format examples...

For the labels, I need to map the `CacheBucket` enum variants to user-friendly names that are clearer than the raw directory names with version suffixes. I'm settling on adding a `name()` method to `CacheBucket` that returns clean, user-friendly labels like "wheels" and "sdists" instead of the version-suffixed internal names, which will make the cache stats output much more readable. in the iterator. So cache stats would only show the buckets in `iter()`.

Now I'm mapping out the exact changes needed: I'll add the `CacheStats` feature flag to the preview features enum with its string representation, add a user-friendly `name()` method to `CacheBucket` that strips version suffixes, and introduce a new `Stats` variant to the cache command that will handle displaying these statistics.

Setting up the implementation files...

Now I'm thinking through the output format for the `cache stats` command. I want to display cache bucket sizes in a clean table with per-bucket rows and a total line at the end, supporting both raw byte counts and human-readable formats like MiB for easier interpretation.

For the bucket labels, I'm considering whether to add a display method to `CacheBucket` itself or keep it localized to the stats module. Since this is really a presentation concern for the CLI output, I'm leaning toward defining the name mapping directly in `cache_stats.rs` rather than polluting the public API. Though I notice `CacheBucket::to_str()` already exists for Display purposes, so I need to think about whether that's the right place or if I should use a separate mapping function.

For calculating sizes, I'll use `diskus::DiskUsage` on each bucket's individual path, handling missing directories by returning zero. Looking at the bucket names to figure out column alignment, the longest ones are "source-distributions" at 20 characters and "environments" at 12, so I'll target around 20 characters of padding for the label column to keep things readable.

For the output format, I'm thinking a simple two-column layout with labels and sizes aligned, similar to how `cache size` displays its output. I'll need to create a `StatsArgs` struct separate from `SizeArgs` to keep things extensible, and I'll respect the `--human` flag for formatting. One thing to note: `CacheBucket::iter()` doesn't include `Python`, so I'll stick with iterating only over the buckets that method returns, matching the existing pattern.

For buckets that don't exist, I'll show "0" or "0B" to give a complete picture. I'll check if each bucket directory exists before calling `DiskUsage` on it, similar to how `cache_size.rs` handles the root cache directory. Since we need per-bucket sizes, I'll make separate `DiskUsage` calls for each bucket rather than trying to combine them, which lets `diskus` properly handle things like hard links.

There's a potential issue with double-counting since `archive-v0` is symlinked from other bucketsŌĆöif we measure each bucket separately, hard-linked files could be counted multiple times. But for a stats display showing which buckets are large, this approximation is acceptable. For the total, I should either sum the individual bucket sizes or compute it with a single `DiskUsage` call over the root to stay consistent with what `cache size` reports. sum of individual buckets would be slightly inconsistent. I'll compute it separately (from the root) to match `cache size`, and note that the sum of per-bucket sizes may not exactly equal the total due to hard links and other factors. input.content:
# Plan: Add `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` already shows the total cache size, but gives no breakdown of
which cache buckets (wheels, sdists, git, etc.) are consuming space. This plan
adds a new `uv cache stats` subcommand that prints a per-bucket table with a
total line at the end, supporting the same `--human` flag as `cache size`.
The command is gated behind a `CacheStats` preview feature, following the same
pattern as `CacheSize`.

---

## Critical files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs` | `PreviewFeature` enum (add `CacheStats`) |
| `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` | CLI arg definitions (`CacheCommand`, new `StatsArgs`) |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` | **NEW** ŌĆō implementation |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs` | Module wiring |
| `crates/uv/src/lib.rs` | Command dispatch |
| `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs` | Test helpers |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs` | **NEW** ŌĆō integration tests |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs` | Test module registration |

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1 ŌĆō Register `CacheStats` as a preview feature
**File:** `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`

a. In the `PreviewFeature` enum (line 197), add after `ProjectDirectoryMustExist`:
```rust
    CacheStats = 1 << 28,
```

b. In `as_str()` (after `"project-directory-must-exist"` arm, line 230):
```rust
    Self::CacheStats => "cache-stats",
```

c. In `FromStr` (after `"project-directory-must-exist"` arm, line 277):
```rust
    "cache-stats" => Self::CacheStats,
```

---

### Step 2 ŌĆō Add CLI args
**File:** `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`

a. In `CacheCommand` enum (after the `Size(SizeArgs)` arm at line 904), add:
```rust
    /// Show a per-bucket breakdown of cache disk usage.
    ///
    /// Displays the disk usage of each cache bucket (wheels, source
    /// distributions, Git repositories, etc.) and a total at the end.
    /// By default, outputs sizes in raw bytes; use `--human` for
    /// human-readable output.
    Stats(StatsArgs),
```

b. After the existing `SizeArgs` struct (line 952), add:
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
pub struct StatsArgs {
    /// Display cache sizes in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB`
    /// instead of raw bytes).
    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
    pub human: bool,
}
```

---

### Step 3 ŌĆō Create `cache_stats.rs` implementation
**File:** `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` *(new file)*

Key design points:
- Same function signature shape as `cache_size`: `(cache, human_readable, printer, preview)`.
- Call `preview.is_enabled(PreviewFeature::CacheStats)` and emit the same
  experimental warning if not enabled.
- Use `CacheBucket::iter()` to enumerate the 10 buckets.
- For each bucket, call `cache.bucket(bucket)` to get its path, then measure
  with `DiskUsage::new(vec![path]).count_ignoring_errors()` if the directory
  exists, or use `0` otherwise.
- Define a local `fn bucket_label(b: CacheBucket) -> &'static str` that maps
  each variant to a short human name without version suffix:
  - `Wheels` ŌåÆ `"wheels"`, `SourceDistributions` ŌåÆ `"source-distributions"`,
    `FlatIndex` ŌåÆ `"flat-index"`, `Git` ŌåÆ `"git"`,
    `Interpreter` ŌåÆ `"interpreter"`, `Simple` ŌåÆ `"simple"`,
    `Archive` ŌåÆ `"archive"`, `Builds` ŌåÆ `"builds"`,
    `Environments` ŌåÆ `"environments"`, `Binaries` ŌåÆ `"binaries"`.
- Collect `(label, bytes)` pairs; sum bytes for a total.
- Compute the column width as `max(label lengths, "total".len())`.
- Print each row left-padded to that width, then the `total` row.
- Raw format: `"{label:<width$}  {bytes}"`.
- Human format: `"{label:<width$}  {bytes:.1}{unit}"` using
  `human_readable_bytes(bytes)`.

Imports to reuse: `DiskUsage` (from `diskus`), `human_readable_bytes` (from
`crate::commands`), `CacheBucket` (from `uv_cache`), `PreviewFeature` (from
`uv_preview`), `warn_user!` (from `uv_warnings`).

---

### Step 4 ŌĆō Wire up the module
**File:** `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`

Add after the `cache_size` lines:
```rust
pub(crate) use cache_stats::cache_stats;   // line ~21
// ŌĆ”
mod cache_stats;                            // line ~84
```

---

### Step 5 ŌĆō Add dispatch arm
**File:** `crates/uv/src/lib.rs`

After the existing `CacheCommand::Size` arm (line 1181), add:
```rust
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Stats(args),
}) => commands::cache_stats(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
```

---

### Step 6 ŌĆō Add test helpers
**File:** `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`

a. After `cache_size()` (line ~1649), add:
```rust
/// Create a `uv cache stats` command.
pub fn cache_stats(&self) -> Command {
    let mut command = self.new_command();
    command.arg("cache").arg("stats");
    self.add_shared_options(&mut command, false);
    command
}
```

b. After `with_filtered_cache_size()` (line ~251), add:
```rust
/// Filter per-bucket size values in `cache stats` output.
#[must_use]
pub fn with_filtered_cache_stats(mut self) -> Self {
    // Raw: "wheels            123456789"
    self.filters.push((
        r"(?m)(\s+)\d+$".to_string(),
        "  [SIZE]".to_string(),
    ));
    // Human-readable: "wheels            117.7 MiB"
    self.filters.push((
        r"(?m)(\s+)\d+(\.\d+)? [KMGT]i?B$".to_string(),
        "  [SIZE]".to_string(),
    ));
    self
}
```

---

### Step 7 ŌĆō Create integration tests
**File:** `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs` *(new file)*

Model tests on `cache_size.rs`. Three tests:

1. **`cache_stats_empty_raw`** ŌĆō clean cache, run `cache stats --preview`;
   snapshot the exact output (all zeros). The bucket table with `0` for every
   bucket and total is stable.

2. **`cache_stats_with_packages_raw`** ŌĆō install `iniconfig`, run
   `cache stats --preview`; use `context.with_filtered_cache_stats()` to
   normalize size values. Verify structure: each expected bucket label is
   present, `total` line appears.

3. **`cache_stats_with_packages_human`** ŌĆō install `iniconfig`, run
   `cache stats --preview --human`; same filter, verify human-readable format
   appears.

---

### Step 8 ŌĆō Register test module
**File:** `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`

After the `cache_size` block (line 27), add:
```rust
#[cfg(all(feature = "test-python", feature = "test-pypi"))]
mod cache_stats;
```

---

## Verification

```bash
# Smoke-test the new subcommand (after build):
uv cache stats --preview
uv cache stats --preview --human

# Run the new integration tests specifically:
cargo test --test it --features test-python,test-pypi cache_stats

# Ensure existing cache tests are unaffected:
cargo test --test it --features test-python,test-pypi cache_size
cargo test --test it --features test-python,test-pypi cache_clean
cargo test --test it --features test-python,test-pypi cache_prune
```

# Plan: Add `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` already shows the total cache size, but gives no breakdown of
which cache buckets (wheels, sdists, git, etc.) are consuming space. This plan
adds a new `uv cache stats` subcommand that prints a per-bucket table with a
total line at the end, supporting the same `--human` flag as `cache size`.
The command is gated behind a `CacheStats` preview feature, following the same
pattern as `CacheSize`.

---

## Critical files

| File                                    | Role                                                  |
| --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`          | `PreviewFeature` enum (add `CacheStats`)              |
| `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`              | CLI arg definitions (`CacheCommand`, new `StatsArgs`) |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` | **NEW** ŌĆō implementation                              |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`         | Module wiring                                         |
| `crates/uv/src/lib.rs`                  | Command dispatch                                      |
| `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`             | Test helpers                                          |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`     | **NEW** ŌĆō integration tests                           |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`            | Test module registration                              |

---

## Step-by-step implementation

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` already shows the total cache size, but gives no breakdown of
which cache buckets (wheels, sdists, git, etc.) are consuming space. This plan
adds a new `uv cache stats` subcommand that prints a per-bucket table with a
total line at the end, supporting the same `--human` flag as `cache size`.
The command is gated behind a `CacheStats` preview feature, following the same
pattern as `CacheSize`.

---

## Critical files

| File                                    | Role                                                  |
| --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`          | `PreviewFeature` enum (add `CacheStats`)              |
| `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`              | CLI arg definitions (`CacheCommand`, new `StatsArgs`) |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` | **NEW** ŌĆō implementation                              |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`         | Module wiring                                         |
| `crates/uv/src/lib.rs`                  | Command dispatch                                      |
| `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`             | Test helpers                                          |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`     | **NEW** ŌĆō integration tests                           |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`            | Test module registration                              |

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1 ŌĆō Register `CacheStats` as a preview feature

**File:** `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`

a. In the `PreviewFeature` enum (line 197), add after `ProjectDirectoryMustExist`:

```rust
    CacheStats = 1 << 28,
```

b. In `as_str()` (after `"project-directory-must-exist"` arm, line 230):

```rust
    Self::CacheStats => "cache-stats",
```

c. In `FromStr` (after `"project-directory-must-exist"` arm, line 277):

```rust
    "cache-stats" => Self::CacheStats,
```

---

### Step 2 ŌĆō Add CLI args

    /// Show a per-bucket breakdown of cache disk usage.

    /// Displays the disk usage of each cache bucket (wheels, source
    /// distributions, Git repositories, etc.) and a total at the end.
    /// By default, outputs sizes in raw bytes; use `--human` for
    /// human-readable output.

```

b. After the existing `SizeArgs` struct (line 952), add:

```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
pub struct StatsArgs {

- Define a local `fn bucket_label(b: CacheBucket) -> &'static str` that maps
  each variant to a short human name without version suffix:
  - `Wheels` ŌåÆ `"wheels"`, `SourceDistributions` ŌåÆ `"source-distributions"`,
    `FlatIndex` ŌåÆ `"flat-index"`, `Git` ŌåÆ `"git"`,
    `Interpreter` ŌåÆ `"interpreter"`, `Simple` ŌåÆ `"simple"`,
    `Archive` ŌåÆ `"archive"`, `Builds` ŌåÆ `"builds"`,
    `Environments` ŌåÆ `"environments"`, `Binaries` ŌåÆ `"binaries"`.
- Collect `(label, bytes)` pairs; sum bytes for a total.
- Compute the column width as `max(label lengths, "total".len())`.
- Print each row left-padded to that width, then the `total` row.
- Raw format: `"{label:<width$}  {bytes}"`.
- Human format: `"{label:<width$}  {bytes:.1}{unit}"` using

impl PreviewFeature {
    /// Returns the string representation of a single preview feature flag.
    fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::PythonInstallDefault => "python-install-default",
            Self::PythonUpgrade => "python-upgrade",
            Self::JsonOutput => "json-output",
            Self::Pylock => "pylock",

            Self::RelocatableEnvsDefault => "relocatable-envs-default",
            Self::PublishRequireNormalized => "publish-require-normalized",
            Self::Audit => "audit",
            Self::ProjectDirectoryMustExist => "project-directory-must-exist",
        }
    }
}

impl Display for PreviewFeature {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}", self.as_str())
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Error, Clone)]
#[error("Unknown feature flag")]
pub struct PreviewFeatureParseError;

impl FromStr for PreviewFeature {
    type Err = PreviewFeatureParseError;

    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
        Ok(match s {
            "python-install-default" => Self::PythonInstallDefault,
            "python-upgrade" => Self::PythonUpgrade,
            "json-output" => Self::JsonOutput,
            "pylock" => Self::Pylock,
            "add-bounds" => Self::AddBounds,
            "package-conflicts" => Self::PackageConflicts,
            "extra-build-dependencies" => Self::ExtraBuildDependencies,
            "detect-module-conflicts" => Self::DetectModuleConflicts,
            "format" => Self::Format,
            "native-auth" => Self::NativeAuth,
            "s3-endpoint" => Self::S3Endpoint,
            "gcs-endpoint" => Self::GcsEndpoint,
            "cache-size" => Self::CacheSize,
            "init-project-flag" => Self::InitProjectFlag,
            "workspace-metadata" => Self::WorkspaceMetadata,
            "workspace-dir" => Self::WorkspaceDir,
            "workspace-list" => Self::WorkspaceList,
            "sbom-export" => Self::SbomExport,
            "auth-helper" => Self::AuthHelper,
            "direct-publish" => Self::DirectPublish,
            "target-workspace-discovery" => Self::TargetWorkspaceDiscovery,
            "metadata-json" => Self::MetadataJson,
            "adjust-ulimit" => Self::AdjustUlimit,
            "special-conda-env-names" => Self::SpecialCondaEnvNames,
            "relocatable-envs-default" => Self::RelocatableEnvsDefault,
            "publish-require-normalized" => Self::PublishRequireNormalized,
            "audit" => Self::Audit,
            "project-directory-must-exist" => Self::ProjectDirectoryMustExist,
            _ => return Err(PreviewFeatureParseError),
        })
    }
}
